testcase.failure:
mock = <MagicMock name='request' id='4584894352'> engine_url = 'http://localhost/engine-rest' MyRequest = <class 'tests.base.conftest.MyRequest.<locals>._MyRequest'>
@unittest.mock.patch('httpx.Client.request') def test_camundarequest_session(mock, engine_url, MyRequest): request = MyRequest(url=engine_url) auth = BasicAuth(username='Jane', password='password')
session = Client() session.auth = auth request.session = session
request()
assert mock.called > assert request.session.auth.username == 'Jane' ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'BasicAuth' object has no attribute 'username'
tests/base/test_camundarequest.py:52: AttributeError

failure.message: TypeError: Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'.

testcase.failure:
mock = <MagicMock name='request' id='4586203056'> engine_url = 'http://localhost/engine-rest'
@unittest.mock.patch('requests.Session.request') def test_variablesupdate_binary_calls_requests(mock, engine_url): update_var = pycamunda.processinst.VariablesUpdate( url=engine_url, process_instance_id='anId', var_name='aVar', value=io.StringIO('myfile'), type_='Bytes' ) > update_var()
tests/processinst/test_variablesupdate.py:76: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pycamunda/processinst.py:778: in __call__ response = super().__call__(pycamunda.base.RequestMethod.POST, *args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pycamunda/base.py:57: in __call__ response = httpx.request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_api.py:109: in request return client.request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_client.py:812: in request request = self.build_request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_client.py:378: in build_request return Request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_models.py:408: in __init__ headers, stream = encode_request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_content.py:212: in encode_request return encode_multipart_data(data or {}, files, boundary) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_content.py:155: in encode_multipart_data multipart = MultipartStream(data=data, files=files, boundary=boundary) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:242: in __init__ self.fields = list(self._iter_fields(data, files)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:256: in _iter_fields yield FileField(name=name, value=value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <httpx._multipart.FileField object at 0x111552650>, name = 'data' value = <_io.StringIO object at 0x114182560>
def __init__(self, name: str, value: FileTypes) -> None: self.name = name
fileobj: FileContent
headers: dict[str, str] = {} content_type: str | None = None
# This large tuple based API largely mirror's requests' API # It would be good to think of better APIs for this that we could # include in httpx 2.0 since variable length tuples(especially of 4 elements) # are quite unwieldly if isinstance(value, tuple): if len(value) == 2: # neither the 3rd parameter (content_type) nor the 4th (headers) # was included filename, fileobj = value elif len(value) == 3: filename, fileobj, content_type = value else: # all 4 parameters included filename, fileobj, content_type, headers = value # type: ignore else: filename = Path(str(getattr(value, "name", "upload"))).name fileobj = value
if content_type is None: content_type = _guess_content_type(filename)
has_content_type_header = any("content-type" in key.lower() for key in headers) if content_type is not None and not has_content_type_header: # note that unlike requests, we ignore the content_type provided in the 3rd # tuple element if it is also included in the headers requests does # the opposite (it overwrites the headerwith the 3rd tuple element) headers["Content-Type"] = content_type
if isinstance(fileobj, io.StringIO): > raise TypeError( "Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'." ) E TypeError: Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'.
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:159: TypeError

testcase.name: test_variablesupdate_binary_raises_pycamunda_exception

failure.message: TypeError: Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'.

testcase.failure:
engine_url = 'http://localhost/engine-rest'
@unittest.mock.patch('requests.Session.request', raise_requests_exception_mock) def test_variablesupdate_binary_raises_pycamunda_exception(engine_url): update_var = pycamunda.processinst.VariablesUpdate( url=engine_url, process_instance_id='anId', var_name='aVar', value=io.StringIO('myfile'), type_='Bytes' ) with pytest.raises(pycamunda.PyCamundaException): > update_var()
tests/processinst/test_variablesupdate.py:105: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pycamunda/processinst.py:778: in __call__ response = super().__call__(pycamunda.base.RequestMethod.POST, *args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pycamunda/base.py:57: in __call__ response = httpx.request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_api.py:109: in request return client.request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_client.py:812: in request request = self.build_request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_client.py:378: in build_request return Request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_models.py:408: in __init__ headers, stream = encode_request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_content.py:212: in encode_request return encode_multipart_data(data or {}, files, boundary) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_content.py:155: in encode_multipart_data multipart = MultipartStream(data=data, files=files, boundary=boundary) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:242: in __init__ self.fields = list(self._iter_fields(data, files)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:256: in _iter_fields yield FileField(name=name, value=value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <httpx._multipart.FileField object at 0x111606330>, name = 'data' value = <_io.StringIO object at 0x114180310>
def __init__(self, name: str, value: FileTypes) -> None: self.name = name
fileobj: FileContent
headers: dict[str, str] = {} content_type: str | None = None
# This large tuple based API largely mirror's requests' API # It would be good to think of better APIs for this that we could # include in httpx 2.0 since variable length tuples(especially of 4 elements) # are quite unwieldly if isinstance(value, tuple): if len(value) == 2: # neither the 3rd parameter (content_type) nor the 4th (headers) # was included filename, fileobj = value elif len(value) == 3: filename, fileobj, content_type = value else: # all 4 parameters included filename, fileobj, content_type, headers = value # type: ignore else: filename = Path(str(getattr(value, "name", "upload"))).name fileobj = value
if content_type is None: content_type = _guess_content_type(filename)
has_content_type_header = any("content-type" in key.lower() for key in headers) if content_type is not None and not has_content_type_header: # note that unlike requests, we ignore the content_type provided in the 3rd # tuple element if it is also included in the headers requests does # the opposite (it overwrites the headerwith the 3rd tuple element) headers["Content-Type"] = content_type
if isinstance(fileobj, io.StringIO): > raise TypeError( "Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'." ) E TypeError: Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'.
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:159: TypeError

failure.message: TypeError: Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'.

testcase.failure:
mock = <MagicMock name='request' id='4583564224'> engine_url = 'http://localhost/engine-rest'
@unittest.mock.patch('requests.Session.request') def test_localvariablesupdate_binary_calls_requests(mock, engine_url): update_var = pycamunda.task.LocalVariablesUpdate( url=engine_url, task_id='anId', var_name='aVar', value=io.StringIO('myfile'), type_='Bytes' ) > update_var()
tests/task/test_localvariablesupdate.py:54: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pycamunda/task.py:1148: in __call__ response = super().__call__(pycamunda.base.RequestMethod.POST, *args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pycamunda/base.py:57: in __call__ response = httpx.request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_api.py:109: in request return client.request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_client.py:812: in request request = self.build_request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_client.py:378: in build_request return Request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_models.py:408: in __init__ headers, stream = encode_request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_content.py:212: in encode_request return encode_multipart_data(data or {}, files, boundary) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_content.py:155: in encode_multipart_data multipart = MultipartStream(data=data, files=files, boundary=boundary) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:242: in __init__ self.fields = list(self._iter_fields(data, files)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:256: in _iter_fields yield FileField(name=name, value=value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <httpx._multipart.FileField object at 0x1115d2140>, name = 'data' value = <_io.StringIO object at 0x11458e7a0>
def __init__(self, name: str, value: FileTypes) -> None: self.name = name
fileobj: FileContent
headers: dict[str, str] = {} content_type: str | None = None
# This large tuple based API largely mirror's requests' API # It would be good to think of better APIs for this that we could # include in httpx 2.0 since variable length tuples(especially of 4 elements) # are quite unwieldly if isinstance(value, tuple): if len(value) == 2: # neither the 3rd parameter (content_type) nor the 4th (headers) # was included filename, fileobj = value elif len(value) == 3: filename, fileobj, content_type = value else: # all 4 parameters included filename, fileobj, content_type, headers = value # type: ignore else: filename = Path(str(getattr(value, "name", "upload"))).name fileobj = value
if content_type is None: content_type = _guess_content_type(filename)
has_content_type_header = any("content-type" in key.lower() for key in headers) if content_type is not None and not has_content_type_header: # note that unlike requests, we ignore the content_type provided in the 3rd # tuple element if it is also included in the headers requests does # the opposite (it overwrites the headerwith the 3rd tuple element) headers["Content-Type"] = content_type
if isinstance(fileobj, io.StringIO): > raise TypeError( "Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'." ) E TypeError: Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'.
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:159: TypeError

testcase.name: test_localvariablesupdate_binary_raises_pycamunda_exception

failure.message: TypeError: Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'.

testcase.failure:
engine_url = 'http://localhost/engine-rest'
@unittest.mock.patch('requests.Session.request', raise_requests_exception_mock) def test_localvariablesupdate_binary_raises_pycamunda_exception(engine_url): update_var = pycamunda.task.LocalVariablesUpdate( url=engine_url, task_id='anId', var_name='aVar', value=io.StringIO('myfile'), type_='Bytes' ) with pytest.raises(pycamunda.PyCamundaException): > update_var()
tests/task/test_localvariablesupdate.py:74: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pycamunda/task.py:1148: in __call__ response = super().__call__(pycamunda.base.RequestMethod.POST, *args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pycamunda/base.py:57: in __call__ response = httpx.request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_api.py:109: in request return client.request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_client.py:812: in request request = self.build_request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_client.py:378: in build_request return Request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_models.py:408: in __init__ headers, stream = encode_request( .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_content.py:212: in encode_request return encode_multipart_data(data or {}, files, boundary) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_content.py:155: in encode_multipart_data multipart = MultipartStream(data=data, files=files, boundary=boundary) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:242: in __init__ self.fields = list(self._iter_fields(data, files)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:256: in _iter_fields yield FileField(name=name, value=value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <httpx._multipart.FileField object at 0x111617850>, name = 'data' value = <_io.StringIO object at 0x11458da20>
def __init__(self, name: str, value: FileTypes) -> None: self.name = name
fileobj: FileContent
headers: dict[str, str] = {} content_type: str | None = None
# This large tuple based API largely mirror's requests' API # It would be good to think of better APIs for this that we could # include in httpx 2.0 since variable length tuples(especially of 4 elements) # are quite unwieldly if isinstance(value, tuple): if len(value) == 2: # neither the 3rd parameter (content_type) nor the 4th (headers) # was included filename, fileobj = value elif len(value) == 3: filename, fileobj, content_type = value else: # all 4 parameters included filename, fileobj, content_type, headers = value # type: ignore else: filename = Path(str(getattr(value, "name", "upload"))).name fileobj = value
if content_type is None: content_type = _guess_content_type(filename)
has_content_type_header = any("content-type" in key.lower() for key in headers) if content_type is not None and not has_content_type_header: # note that unlike requests, we ignore the content_type provided in the 3rd # tuple element if it is also included in the headers requests does # the opposite (it overwrites the headerwith the 3rd tuple element) headers["Content-Type"] = content_type
if isinstance(fileobj, io.StringIO): > raise TypeError( "Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'." ) E TypeError: Multipart file uploads require 'io.BytesIO', not 'io.StringIO'.
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/httpx/_multipart.py:159: TypeError
